name: historical_notes
Siege Lines at Dyrrhachium: Compressed Dyrrhachium siege lines: Caesar's blockade works are stretched around Pompey's coastal camp; Pompey's better-supplied army exploits a weak sector and turns Caesar's failed counterattack into a Roman defeat.

name: ROMAN
column4: hold the overextended contravallation and contain any Pompeian breakout long enough to withdraw in order
column5: prevent the breach from becoming a rout

name: POMPEIAN
column4: concentrate on the weak southern sector and drive through Caesar's siege works with superior numbers
column5: break the blockade and force Caesar away from Dyrrhachium
